{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_019bGd5scmknKpPn2DDVbfuA","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01H7mwGkoouNemUSeK6fMcst","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cd sr.loc[sr.groupby('store_id')['n'].idxmax()][['store_id','rep_id']].rename(columns={'rep_id':'primary_rep'})\nprint(\"\\n# stores each rep is PRIMARY for: describe\")\nprint(primary['primary_rep'].value_counts().describe())\nprint(\"counts per rep (head):\")\nprint(primary['primary_rep'].value_counts().sort_index().head(10))\n\n# Team structure: the set store[['store_id','region']].merge(sets.rename('team').reset_index(), on='store_id')\nprint(\"\\nsample teams by region:\")\ntt = store_team.groupby('region')['team'].apply(lambda x: x.value_counts().index.tolist()[:2])\nfor reg in sorted(store_team.region.unique())[:5]:\n    teams = store_team[store_team.region==reg]['team'].unique()\n    print(f\"  {reg}: {len(teams)} teams, {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_014UuWTWhzyXytsYw1CjGkB2","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01D15EDnkBAzd17wjArvhVnM","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cd /app\npython3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, glob, numpy as np\n\n# 1. Sales per store (annual)\nagg=None\nfor f in sorted(glob.glob('sales_transaction_*.csv')):\n    df=pd.read_csv(f, usecols=['store_id','units_sold','revenue','promo_price'])\n    df['promo_flag']=(df['promo_price']>0).astype(int)\n    g=df.groupby('store_id').agg(units=('units_sold','sum'),revenue=('revenue','sum'),\n                                 n_rows=('units_sold','size'),promo_rows=('promo_flag','sum'))\n    agg=g if agg is None else agg.add(g,fill_value=0)\nagg=agg.reset_index()\n\n# 2. Traffic per store\ntr=None\nfor f in sorted(glob.glob('store_traffic_*.csv')):\n    df=pd.read_csv(f,usecols=['store_id','customer_count'])\n    g=df.groupby('store_id').agg(traffic=('customer_count','sum'),traffic_days=('customer_count','size'))\n    tr=g if tr is None else tr.add(g,fill_value=0)\ntr=tr.reset_index()\n\n# 3. Inventory out-of-stock per store (rep's availability work -- keep as diagnostic, NOT control)\ninv=None\nfor f in sorted(glob.glob('inventory_status_*.csv')):\n    df=pd.read_csv(f,usecols=['store_id','out_of_stock','stock_units'])\n    df['oos']=df['out_of_stock'].astype(int)\n    g=df.groupby('store_id').agg(oos_rows=('oos','sum'),inv_rows=('oos','size'),stock=('stock_units','sum'))\n    inv=g if inv is None else inv.add(g,fill_value=0)\ninv=inv.reset_index(); inv['oos_rate']=inv.oos_rows/inv.inv_rows\n\n# 4. Competition per store (via visits)\nvis=pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f,usecols=['visit_id','store_id','rep_id','duration_minutes','completed_visit']) for f in sorted(glob.glob('store_visit_*.csv'))],ignore_index=True)\ncomp=pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('competitor_audit_*.csv'))],ignore_index=True)\ncomp=comp.merge(vis[['visit_id','store_id']],on='visit_id',how='left')\ncompg=comp.groupby('store_id').agg(comp_facings=('competitor_facings','mean'),\n                                   comp_promo=('competitor_promotion','mean')).reset_index()\n\n# 5. store_promotion intensity per store\nsp=pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('store_promotion_*.csv'))],ignore_index=True)\nspg=sp.groupby('store_id').agg(n_promos=('store_promotion_id','size'),\n                               display_active=('display_active','mean')).reset_index()\n\n# 6. Primary rep per store\nsr=vis.groupby(['store_id','rep_id']).size().rename('n').reset_index()\nprimary=sr.loc[sr.groupby('store_id')['n'].idxmax()][['store_id','rep_id']].rename(columns={'rep_id':'primary_rep'})\n\n# Merge master\nstore=pd.read_csv('store_001.csv')\nm=store.merge(agg,on='store_id').merge(tr,on='store_id').merge(inv,on='store_id')\\\n       .merge(compg,on='store_id',how='left').merge(spg,on='store_id',how='left')\\\n       .merge(primary,on='store_id')\nm['n_promos']=m['n_promos'].fillna(0); m['display_active']=m['display_active'].fillna(0)\nm.to_csv('/tmp/master_store.csv',index=False)\nprint(\"master shape\",m.shape)\nprint(m[['store_size_sqm','units','revenue','traffic','oos_rate','comp_facings','promo_share','n_promos']].describe().round(3))\nprint(\"\\nstore_type counts:\",m.store_type.value_counts().to_dict())\nprint(\"location counts:\",m.location.value_counts().to_dict())\nEOF","description":"Build master store-level table"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4192,"cache_read_input_tokens":40634,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4192,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":45,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"223d23e9-2c3b-43a8-a420-1829b4264f86","uuid":"b633cd6f-cfd9-49ea-971c-9659a6edb795","request_id":"req_011Cbf17bsHnxM6wGCS1fboS"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01MwTJHjThA8oFjE5wFcQVmt","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01R5QW3afvwVHuWyegkCQz1H","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cd /app\npython3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np\nimport statsmodels.formula.api as smf\nm=pd.read_csv('/tmp/master_store.csv')\nm['lu']=np.log(m['units']); m['lsize']=np.log(m['store_size_sqm']); m['ltr']=np.log(m['traffic'])\n\n# Baseline model: external factors ONLY (no rep, no oos/display which are rep outputs)\nf='lu ~ ltr + lsize + C(store_type) + C(location) + C(region) + comp_facings + comp_promo + n_promos'\nmod=smf.ols(f,data=m).fit()\nprint(\"R2=\",round(mod.rsquared,4),\" n=\",int(mod.nobs))\nm['resid']=mod.resid\n\n# Group residuals by primary rep (5 stores each)\ng=m.groupby('primary_rep')['resid'].agg(['mean','std','count']).sort_values('mean')\nprint(\"\\n--- Lowest 15 reps by mean residual (primary-rep attribution) ---\")\nprint(g.head(15).round(4))\nprint(\"\\n--- Highest 5 ---\")\nprint(g.tail(5).round(4))\nprint(\"\\nResidual std (store-level):\", round(m['resid'].std(),4))\nprint(\"Rep-mean residual distribution:\")\nprint(g['mean'].describe().round(4))\nEOF","description":"Baseline OLS and residuals by primary rep"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1030,"cache_read_input_tokens":48361,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1030,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":41,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"223d23e9-2c3b-43a8-a420-1829b4264f86","uuid":"95b0f781-a4dc-46cd-a801-da4711f2a6d2","request_id":"req_011Cbf1Aix1vrWaHritCoBCz"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01LLaUwHjjWBga7iYiy1PGQS","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Gkqhv5GHMr2q8pYKKDnN75","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cd /app\npython3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, glob\nimport statsmodels.formula.api as smf\nimport statsmodels.api as sm\n\nm=pd.read_csv('/tmp/master_store.csv')\nm['lu']=np.log(m['units']); m['lsize']=np.log(m['store_size_sqm']); m['ltr']=np.log(m['traffic'])\nf='lu ~ ltr + lsize + C(store_type) + C(location) + C(region) + comp_facings + comp_promo + n_promos'\nmod=smf.ols(f,data=m).fit()\nm['resid']=mod.resid\n\n# 1. ANOVA: does primary_rep explain residual?\nimport scipy.stats as st\ngroups=[v['resid'].values for _,v in m.groupby('primary_rep')]\nF,p=st.f_oneway(*groups)\nprint(f\"ANOVA residual ~ primary_rep: F={F:.3f}, p={p:.4g}\")\n\n# 2. Visit-weight matrix\nvis=pd.concat([pd.read_csv(fn,usecols=['store_id','rep_id']) for fn in sorted(glob.glob('store_visit_*.csv'))],ignore_index=True)\nsr=vis.groupby(['store_id','rep_id']).size().rename('n').reset_index()\nsr['w']=sr['n']/sr.groupby('store_id')['n'].transform('sum')\nW=sr.pivot_table(index='store_id',columns='rep_id',values='w',fill_value=0.0)\nW=W.reindex(m.sort_values('store_id')['store_id']).fillna(0.0)\nms=m.sort_values('store_id').reset_index(drop=True)\ny=ms['resid'].values\n# region FE design + weighted rep matrix (drop one rep per region for identifiability via region intercepts)\nreg_d=pd.get_dummies(ms['region'],prefix='reg',drop_first=True).astype(float)\nX=pd.concat([pd.Series(1.0,index=ms.index,name='const'),reg_d.reset_index(drop=True),\n             W.reset_index(drop=True).add_prefix('rep_')],axis=1)\n# rank-deficient (rows of W sum to 1 ~ const within region) -> use pinv least squares\nXv=X.values.astype(float)\nbeta,_,rank,_=np.linalg.lstsq(Xv,y,rcond=None)\nprint(\"design rank\",rank,\"of\",Xv.shape[1])\nrep_cols=[c for c in X.columns if c.startswith('rep_')]\ncoef=pd.Series(beta[[X.columns.get_loc(c) for c in rep_cols]], index=[int(c[4:]) for c in rep_cols])\ncoef=coef.sort_values()\nprint(\"\\nLowest 15 rep coefficients (weighted, region-centered):\")\nprint(coef.head(15).round(4))\nprint(\"\\nDistribution of rep coefficients:\")\nprint(coef.describe().round(4))\nEOF","description":"ANOVA and weighted rep regression"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2801,"cache_read_input_tokens":49391,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2801,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":52,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"223d23e9-2c3b-43a8-a420-1829b4264f86","uuid":"6e7553d0-012f-40ef-b436-c89c9d257ebf","request_id":"req_011Cbf1DKiMRiFQSQbxP3XMK"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01YYfJ73ayZsCDvLh7h62vfT","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01DZG9SLeyiW9XqnUVsvw8ak","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cd /app\npython3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, glob\nimport scipy.stats as st\n\nvis=pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f,usecols=['store_id','rep_id']) for f in sorted(glob.glob('store_visit_*.csv'))],ignore_index=True)\nsr=vis.groupby(['store_id','rep_id']).size().rename('n').reset_index()\nprimary=sr.loc[sr.groupby('store_id')['n'].idxmax()][['store_id','rep_id']].rename(columns={'rep_id':'primary_rep'})\n\n# OOS per store\ninv=None\nfor f in sorted(glob.glob('inventory_status_*.csv')):\n    df=pd.read_csv(f,usecols=['store_id','out_of_stock'])\n    df['oos']=df['out_of_stock'].astype(int)\n    g=df.groupby('store_id').agg(oos=('oos','sum'),n=('oos','size'))\n    inv=g if inv is None else inv.add(g,fill_value=0)\ninv=inv.reset_index(); inv['oos_rate']=inv['oos']/inv['n']\ninv=inv.merge(primary,on='store_id')\n\nprint(\"OOS rate overall: mean=%.4f std=%.4f\"%(inv.oos_rate.mean(),inv.oos_rate.std()))\ng=inv.groupby('primary_rep')['oos_rate'].agg(['mean','std','count']).sort_values('mean',ascending=False)\nprint(\"\\nHighest OOS reps (worst availability):\")\nprint(g.head(12).round(4))\nF,p=st.f_oneway(*[v['oos_rate'].values for _,v in inv.groupby('primary_rep')])\nprint(f\"\\nANOVA oos_rate ~ primary_rep: F={F:.3f} p={p:.4g}\")\nprint(\"Distribution of rep-mean OOS:\", g['mean'].describe().round(4).to_dict())\n\n# Order lead time per store\nord_=pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f,parse_dates=['order_date','delivery_date']) for f in sorted(glob.glob('store_order_*.csv'))],ignore_index=True)\nord_['lead']=(ord_['delivery_date']-ord_['order_date']).dt.days\nom=ord_.groupby('store_id')['lead'].mean().reset_index().merge(primary,on='store_id')\ngl=om.groupby('primary_rep')['lead'].mean().sort_values(ascending=False)\nF2,p2=st.f_oneway(*[v['lead'].values for _,v in om.groupby('primary_rep')])\nprint(f\"\\nOrder lead time: overall mean={ord_['lead'].mean():.3f}\")\nprint(f\"ANOVA lead ~ primary_rep: F={F2:.3f} p={p2:.4g}\")\nprint(\"worst (longest lead) reps:\", gl.head(8).round(3).to_dict())\nEOF","description":"Check OOS and order lead time by rep"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4343,"cache_read_input_tokens":56811,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4343,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":28,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"223d23e9-2c3b-43a8-a420-1829b4264f86","uuid":"a31f4b0c-6a4b-4893-a0c1-35edf2c264f5","request_id":"req_011Cbf1LyLyQMHVUXKgwyFx5"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01T3ihhmhhG3kXRNiDUTaeAW","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_0162bEp94Tt8ZGcL8FPyWvkQ","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cd /app\npython3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, glob\nm=pd.read_csv('/tmp/master_store.csv')\nimport statsmodels.formula.api as smf\nm['lu']=np.log(m['units']);m['lsize']=np.log(m['store_size_sqm']);m['ltr']=np.log(m['traffic'])\nmod=smf.ols('lu ~ ltr+lsize+C(store_type)+C(location)+C(region)+comp_facings+comp_promo+n_promos',data=m).fit()\nm['sales_resid']=mod.resid\nprint(\"corr(sales_resid, oos_rate) =\", round(m['sales_resid'].corr(m['oos_rate']),3))\n\n# Look at suspect reps' individual stores: oos_rate and sales_resid per store\nfor rep in [52,82,66,87,49,31,8,3]:\n    sub=m[m.primary_rep==rep][['store_id','oos_rate','sales_resid','units','traffic']].sort_values('oos_rate',ascending=False)\n    print(f\"\\n--- Rep {rep}: 5 primary stores ---\")\n    print(sub.assign(oos_rate=sub.oos_rate.round(3),sales_resid=sub.sales_resid.round(3)).to_string(index=False))\nEOF","description":"Examine suspect reps' stores and OOS-sales link"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":131,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4663,"cache_read_input_tokens":61154,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4663,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":45,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"223d23e9-2c3b-43a8-a420-1829b4264f86","uuid":"35758fdd-6fd9-4dce-b202-57c8641beeb8","request_id":"req_011Cbf1RuTTM5JBMCuoihpSM"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01QyFrckhNgTFHtag8y7Hq2v","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Now the picture sharpens. Let me run a permutation test on the weighted rep-effect estimates and examine consistency across each rep's stores."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4996,"cache_read_input_tokens":69851,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4996,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":45,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"223d23e9-2c3b-43a8-a420-1829b4264f86","uuid":"218eb282-0491-4863-a335-507cc28fa6f9","request_id":"req_011Cbf1ZzHeFj3QxzAfEFh9N"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01CxHxvgatCAUXc8Mjironyx","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01WeGtXSihNG6UNdn1RuAJS9","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cd /app\npython3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, glob\nimport statsmodels.formula.api as smf\nnp.random.seed(0)\nm=pd.read_csv('/tmp/master_store.csv')\nm['lu']=np.log(m['units']);m['lsize']=np.log(m['store_size_sqm']);m['ltr']=np.log(m['traffic'])\nmod=smf.ols('lu ~ ltr+lsize+C(store_type)+C(location)+C(region)+comp_facings+comp_promo+n_promos',data=m).fit()\nm['resid']=mod.resid\n\n# Per-rep primary-store stats + consistency\ng=m.groupby('primary_rep')['resid'].agg(mean='mean',std='std',mn='min',mx='max',\n        n_neg=lambda x:(x<0).sum()).reset_index()\ng['all5neg']=g['n_neg']==5\n# t-stat of rep mean vs 0 using global store resid sd\nsd=m['resid'].std()\ng['z']=g['mean']/(sd/np.sqrt(5))\ng=g.sort_values('mean')\nprint(\"Reps with ALL 5 primary stores negative residual:\",int(g['all5neg'].sum()))\nprint(g[g['all5neg']].sort_values('mean')[['primary_rep','mean','std','mx','z']].round(4).to_string(index=False))\n\n# How many all-5-neg expected by chance? ~ 100 * 0.5^5 = 3.1\nprint(\"\\nExpected all-5-neg by chance (if resid mean 0):\", round(100*0.5**5,2))\n\n# Permutation test: shuffle store->primary_rep WITHIN region, get null dist of min rep-mean\nreg=m.set_index('store_id')['region']\nstore_rep=m[['store_id','primary_rep','region','resid']].copy()\nobs_min=g['mean'].min()\nobs_sorted=g['mean'].values\nnperm=2000\nnull_mins=[]; null_5th=[]\nresid_by_region={r:v['resid'].values for r,v in store_rep.groupby('region')}\n# each region has 25 stores, 5 reps x 5 -> shuffle assignment of 5 reps to 5 store-groups? \n# Simpler: shuffle resid among stores within region, recompute primary-rep means\nprim=store_rep.groupby('primary_rep')\nrep_region=store_rep.groupby('primary_rep')['region'].first()\nfor _ in range(nperm):\n    sh=store_rep.copy()\n    sh['rresid']=sh.groupby('region')['resid'].transform(np.random.permutation)\n    gm=sh.groupby('primary_rep')['rresid'].mean()\n    null_mins.append(gm.min())\n    null_5th.append(np.sort(gm.values)[4])  # 5th lowest\nnull_mins=np.array(null_mins)\nprint(f\"\\nObserved lowest rep-mean = {obs_min:.4f}\")\nprint(f\"Permutation null lowest rep-mean: 1%={np.percentile(null_mins,1):.4f} 5%={np.percentile(null_mins,5):.4f} (these are the most-negative single rep under null)\")\nprint(f\"p-value(observed min): {(null_mins<=obs_min).mean():.4f}\")\n# significance threshold = 5th percentile of null min distribution -> any rep below is family-wise significant\nthr=np.percentile(null_mins,5)\nsig=g[g['mean']<=thr]\nprint(f\"\\nReps below FWER-5% threshold ({thr:.4f}): {sorted(sig['primary_rep'].tolist())}\")\nprint(sig[['primary_rep','mean','std','z','all5neg']].round(4).to_string(index=False))\nEOF","description":"Permutation test and consistency analysis"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4996,"cache_read_input_tokens":69851,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4996,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":45,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"223d23e9-2c3b-43a8-a420-1829b4264f86","uuid":"b961c561-f100-48e8-ac63-5166024a8c7a","request_id":"req_011Cbf1ZzHeFj3QxzAfEFh9N"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01AMjBSUWEMGn2WPEWrgmF9k","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01RTXpJeAvheDrnDrYjEbKXL","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cd /app\npython3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, glob\nvis=pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('store_visit_*.csv'))],ignore_index=True)\nvis['vs']=pd.to_datetime(vis['visit_start'])\n# Per-rep rich features\ng=vis.groupby('rep_id').agg(\n    n_visits=('visit_id','size'),\n    completion=('completed_visit','mean'),\n    avg_dur=('duration_minutes','mean'),\n    total_dur=('duration_minutes','sum'),\n    planned=('planned_visit','mean'),\n    n_stores=('store_id','nunique'),\n)\n# visit type mix\nvt=vis.pivot_table(index='rep_id',columns='visit_type',values='visit_id',aggfunc='size',fill_value=0)\nvt=vt.div(vt.sum(1),axis=0)\ng=g.join(vt)\n# completed-visit duration efficiency: dur per completed visit\ng['dur_per_completed']=g['total_dur']/(vis.groupby('rep_id')['completed_visit'].sum())\n\n# Gap detector: for each feature, sort and find the largest gap in the lower tail\ndef gaps(col):\n    v=np.sort(g[col].values)\n    d=np.diff(v)\n    # normalized gaps\n    dn=d/ (g[col].std())\n    i=np.argmax(dn[:30])  # look in lower portion\n    return v, d, i\nprint(\"Feature distributions (sorted lower tail, looking for gaps):\")\nfor c in g.columns:\n    v=np.sort(g[c].values)\n    d=np.diff(v)\n    sd=g[c].std()\n    # biggest normalized gap and where\n    idx=np.argsort(d)[::-1][:3]\n    gapinfo=[(round(v[i],3),round(v[i+1],3),round(d[i]/sd,2)) for i in sorted(idx)]\n    print(f\"  {c:18s} min={v[0]:.3f} max={v[-1]:.3f} sd={sd:.4f} top-gaps(lo,hi,/sd)={gapinfo}\")\ng.to_csv('/tmp/rep_features.csv')\nEOF","description":"Rich rep features and gap detection"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":7161,"cache_read_input_tokens":74847,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":7161,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":67,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"223d23e9-2c3b-43a8-a420-1829b4264f86","uuid":"a2d78331-511b-40b7-ae09-171d1c40626a","request_id":"req_011Cbf1hiyNKzkdYnWoLugSB"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01J25y8TaT9MwA6YTWRK5WW9","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"No discrete clusters exist in *any* rep work feature — all smoothly distributed. So underperformance is a continuous efficiency parameter, and the signal is weak per-rep. The decisive question: **are rep efficiency effects real or noise?** Let me run an out-of-sample validation — estimate each rep's effect from 4 of their 5 stores (leave-one-out) and test whether it predicts the held-out store, plus whether it transfers to their secondary stores."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":5033,"cache_read_input_tokens":82008,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":5033,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":55,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"223d23e9-2c3b-43a8-a420-1829b4264f86","uuid":"8e52d6ed-edb6-4536-b9dd-6cc85e2559d6","request_id":"req_011Cbf1mbpsCKuk7CR4We3Ke"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01J25y8TaT9MwA6YTWRK5WW9","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Ehtp1ZCdUmD9RPHqFCdvkF","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cd /app\npython3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, glob\nimport statsmodels.formula.api as smf\nimport scipy.stats as st\nm=pd.read_csv('/tmp/master_store.csv')\nm['lu']=np.log(m['units']);m['lsize']=np.log(m['store_size_sqm']);m['ltr']=np.log(m['traffic'])\nmod=smf.ols('lu ~ ltr+lsize+C(store_type)+C(location)+C(region)+comp_facings+comp_promo+n_promos',data=m).fit()\nm['resid']=mod.resid\nm=m.sort_values('store_id').reset_index(drop=True)\n\n# primary effect per rep\nprim_eff=m.groupby('primary_rep')['resid'].mean()\n# Leave-one-out primary effect for each store\ncnt=m.groupby('primary_rep')['resid'].transform('size')\nsm=m.groupby('primary_rep')['resid'].transform('sum')\nm['e_p_loo']=(sm-m['resid'])/(cnt-1)   # mean of OTHER primary stores of this store's primary rep\n\n# TEST 1: does leave-one-out primary effect predict held-out store residual?\nr,p=st.pearsonr(m['e_p_loo'],m['resid'])\nprint(f\"TEST1 (LOO primary effect -> held-out store resid): r={r:.3f}, p={p:.4g}, n={len(m)}\")\n\n# TEST 2: secondary transfer. Build weighted secondary effect using OTHER reps' primary effects\nvis=pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f,usecols=['store_id','rep_id']) for f in sorted(glob.glob('store_visit_*.csv'))],ignore_index=True)\nsr=vis.groupby(['store_id','rep_id']).size().rename('n').reset_index()\nsr['w']=sr['n']/sr.groupby('store_id')['n'].transform('sum')\nprimary=m[['store_id','primary_rep']]\nsr=sr.merge(primary,on='store_id')\nsec=sr[sr['rep_id']!=sr['primary_rep']].copy()  # secondary rep rows\nsec['e_q']=sec['rep_id'].map(prim_eff)   # secondary rep's efficiency estimated from THEIR primary stores\n# renormalize secondary weights, weighted secondary effect per store\nsec_eff=sec.groupby('store_id').apply(lambda d:np.average(d['e_q'],weights=d['w']),include_groups=False)\nm['sec_eff']=m['store_id'].map(sec_eff)\nr2,p2=st.pearsonr(m['sec_eff'],m['resid'])\nprint(f\"TEST2 (secondary reps' efficiency -> store resid, fully out-of-sample): r={r2:.3f}, p={p2:.4g}\")\n\n# Combined regression\nmm=m.dropna(subset=['sec_eff','e_p_loo'])\nreg=smf.ols('resid ~ e_p_loo + sec_eff',data=mm).fit()\nprint(\"\\nCombined OOS regression resid ~ LOO_primary + secondary_effect:\")\nprint(reg.summary().tables[1])\nEOF","description":"Out-of-sample validation of rep effects"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":5033,"cache_read_input_tokens":82008,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":5033,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":55,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"223d23e9-2c3b-43a8-a420-1829b4264f86","uuid":"56a1e547-92ca-43cb-ba13-d827966a0bda","request_id":"req_011Cbf1mbpsCKuk7CR4We3Ke"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_011qNDkrMn4FW8QJf5bARCeJ","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"**Decisive result.** The leave-one-out test shows a rep's effect estimated from 4 of their stores does **not** predict the 5th (r=0.06, p=0.18), and the \"secondary transfer\" is *negative* — a mechanical artifact of within-region centering. **Store sales carry no real rep-efficiency signal.** I've been chasing noise.\n\nLet me pivot to the rep's actual merchandising work, controlled *within store×product* (removing store/product confounds that marginal averages hide) — the rep's audits reflect the shelf state they leave."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":131,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4852,"cache_read_input_tokens":87041,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4852,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":52,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"223d23e9-2c3b-43a8-a420-1829b4264f86","uuid":"498a078d-915e-4b45-bb86-9a3e62f8ab66","request_id":"req_011Cbf1rg31WvUhYWmX31UMW"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_011qNDkrMn4FW8QJf5bARCeJ","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01GXEKuZYLQZ75edMJZh6XuY","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cd /app\npython3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, glob\nimport scipy.stats as st\nvis=pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f,usecols=['visit_id','store_id','rep_id','visit_type','completed_visit']) for f in sorted(glob.glob('store_visit_*.csv'))],ignore_index=True)\nsh=pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('shelf_audit_*.csv'))],ignore_index=True)\nsh=sh.merge(vis,on='visit_id',how='left')\nprint(\"audits:\",len(sh))\n\n# Within (store,product) deviations -> isolate rep effect removing store&product\nfor metric in ['facings','planogram_compliance','endcap_display','checkout_display','promo_stand','pos_materials','shelf_width_cm']:\n    x=sh[metric].astype(float)\n    grpmean=sh.groupby(['store_id','product_id'])[metric].transform('mean')\n    sh[metric+'_dev']=x-grpmean\n# Per-rep mean within-store-product deviation + ANOVA on deviations\nprint(\"\\nPer-rep WITHIN-(store,product) effect (mean deviation). Look for outlier reps:\")\nfor metric in ['planogram_compliance','facings','endcap_display','checkout_display','promo_stand','pos_materials']:\n    dev=sh.groupby('rep_id')[metric+'_dev'].mean()\n    F,p=st.f_oneway(*[v.values for _,v in sh.groupby('rep_id')[metric+'_dev']])\n    lo=dev.sort_values()\n    print(f\"\\n  {metric}: ANOVA F={F:.2f} p={p:.3g} | rep-dev std={dev.std():.4f}\")\n    print(f\"     lowest reps: {[(int(i),round(v,4)) for i,v in lo.head(8).items()]}\")\nEOF","description":"Within store×product rep effect on shelf quality"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":131,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4852,"cache_read_input_tokens":87041,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4852,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":52,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"223d23e9-2c3b-43a8-a420-1829b4264f86","uuid":"03605a8a-0e6d-4656-9292-3c4a0773ddf0","request_id":"req_011Cbf1rg31WvUhYWmX31UMW"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01BguzUKFHH75bdqJRbMwb7h","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01PSBJt7Mhkq9JXX1G58iMBS","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cd /app\npython3 << 'EOF'\nimport pandas as pd, numpy as np, glob\nimport statsmodels.formula.api as smf\nimport scipy.stats as st\nunder={3,8,17,31,45,49,64,68,75,98}\nm=pd.read_csv('/tmp/master_store.csv')\nm['lu']=np.log(m['units']);m['lsize']=np.log(m['store_size_sqm']);m['ltr']=np.log(m['traffic'])\nmod=smf.ols('lu ~ ltr+lsize+C(store_type)+C(location)+C(region)+comp_facings+comp_promo+n_promos',data=m).fit()\nm['resid']=mod.resid\nm['under']=m['primary_rep'].isin(under)\na=m[m.under]['resid']; b=m[~m.under]['resid']\nt,p=st.ttest_ind(a,b)\nprint(f\"Sales residual @ primary stores: underperformers mean={a.mean():.4f} (n={len(a)}) vs others mean={b.mean():.4f} (n={len(b)})\")\nprint(f\"  t={t:.2f}, one-sided p={p/2:.4g}  -> underperformers' stores sell {(np.exp(a.mean()-b.mean())-1)*100:.2f}% less than expected vs peers\")\n\n# Also confirm via OOS / availability tie-in\nprint(f\"\\nOOS rate: underperformers={m[m.under]['oos_rate'].mean():.4f} vs others={m[~m.under]['oos_rate'].mean():.4f}\")\n\nprint(\"\\nFINAL ANSWER (sorted):\", sorted(under))\nEOF","description":"Confirm sales-results tie-in for the 10 reps"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":2,"cache_creation_input_tokens":2764,"cache_read_input_tokens":100733,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":2764,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":43,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"223d23e9-2c3b-43a8-a420-1829b4264f86","uuid":"e8659fa2-6c79-4b67-bb63-90a49bf21502","request_id":"req_011Cbf22CXZt9fNQRgLjL8PZ"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_018XWUzCWLgKgZqjPWrPxGZT","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"I've written the answer. Here's the summary of the analysis.\n\n## Answer\n`{\"answer\": [3, 8, 17, 31, 45, 49, 64, 68, 75, 98]}` — written to `/app/answer.json`.\n\n## How I got there\n\n**Data structure.** 20 regions, each with 5 reps and 25 stores. The 5 reps form a team covering all 25 stores; each rep is the **primary** rep (~65% of visits) for exactly 5 stores. Sales/inventory/orders are at store level (shared by 5 reps), while audits are tied to a rep's specific visits.\n\n**Ruling out the obvious dead-ends:**\n- Raw per-rep work metrics (visit count, completion, duration, display flags) are all smoothly distributed — no outlier group.\n- Store **sales** are almost entirely explained by external factors (log-units ~ log-traffic, R²=0.9997). A leave-one-out test proved the apparent per-rep sales differences are **noise** — a rep's effect from 4 of their stores doesn't predict the 5th.\n- High **out-of-stock** is structurally driven by small store size, not rep skill (a red herring).\n\n**The real signal.** Controlling *within store×product* (removing store and product confounds that marginal averages hide), there's a highly significant rep effect on the rep's core merchandising duties:\n- **facings** (shelf space): F=5.0, p=6e-54\n- **planogram_compliance** (placement-plan implementation): F=3.8, p=2e-34\n\nSorting reps by this deviation produces **exactly 10 reps** sitting −2.3 to −3.0σ below the rest, separated by a **~1.75σ gap** from the 11th. Facings, planogram, and the combined z-score *independently* flag the identical 10, and a temporal split-half (H1 vs H2) confirms it's a stable rep trait (r≈0.66, p~1e-13) — not noise.\n\n**Tie to sales results.** As a group, these 10 reps' primary stores sell **1.2% below expectation** (t=−4.06, p=2.8e-05) after controlling for traffic, store size/type, location, competition, and promotions — confirming the merchandising deficiency shows up in sales, as the prompt frames it.\n\nThese reps significantly underperform on display/shelf-space and product-placement-plan execution — the heart of their job."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":131,"cache_creation_input_tokens":636,"cache_read_input_tokens":105837,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":636,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"223d23e9-2c3b-43a8-a420-1829b4264f86","uuid":"81eacef2-06e0-4ca0-adad-4b5de2488c5d","request_id":"req_011Cbf25EGLCMr93Etoo6u9e"}
